name: Tirumala Venkateswara Temple
description: The Tirumala Venkateswara Temple is a Hindu temple situated in the hill town of Tirumala at Tirupati in Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh, India. The Temple is dedicated to Venkateswara, a form of Vishnu.
history: The temple was constructed over a period of time starting from 300 AD. It is one of the richest temples in the world.
architecture: Dravidian architecture with the main vimana, the Ananda Nilayam, completely plated with gold.
address: S Mada St, Tirumala, Tirupati, Andhra Pradesh 517504
dressCode: Traditional Wear. Men: Dhoti/Kurta. Women: Saree/Chudidar with Dupatta.

faqs.question: Is darshan free?
faqs.answer: Sarva Darshan is free, while Special Darshan costs ₹300.

name: Meenakshi Amman Temple
description: A historic Hindu temple located on the southern bank of the Vaigai River in the temple city of Madurai, Tamil Nadu, India.
history: The temple is at the center of the ancient temple city of Madurai and was built by the Pandyan Emperor.
architecture: Dravidian architecture, famous for its 14 gopurams, painted in bright colors and covered with figures of deities.
dressCode: Strictly traditional. No shorts or sleeveless.

faqs.question: Is there an entry fee?
faqs.answer: General entry is free, but special darshan tickets are available.

name: Shree Jagannath Temple
description: An important Hindu temple dedicated to Jagannath, a form of Vishnu, in Puri in the state of Odisha on the eastern coast of India.
history: The present temple was rebuilt from the 10th century onwards, on the site of an earlier temple, and begun by King Anantavarman Chodaganga Deva.
architecture: Kalinga architecture with a massive vimana topped by the Neelachakra.
deity: Lord Jagannath, Lord Balabhadra, Devi Subhadra
dressCode: Traditional Indian attire. Non-Hindus are not allowed inside.

faqs.question: Can non-Hindus enter?
faqs.answer: No, but they can view the temple from the Raghunandan Library platform.

name: Kashi Vishwanath Temple
description: One of the most famous Hindu temples dedicated to Lord Shiva. It is located in Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, India.
history: The temple has been destroyed and rebuilt several times. The current structure was built on an adjacent site by the Maratha ruler Ahilyabai Holkar of Indore in 1780.
architecture: Nagara style architecture with gold-plated domes.

faqs.question: What are the timing for Mangala Aarti?
faqs.answer: Mangala Aarti happens from 3:00 AM to 4:00 AM daily.

name: Kedarnath Temple
description: Kedarnath Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, located on the Garhwal Himalayan range near the Mandakini river in Uttarakhand.
history: The temple was initially built by Pandavas and is one of the twelve Jyotirlingas, the holiest Hindu shrines of Shiva.
architecture: The temple is built of massive, evenly cut grey stone slabs with a wooden roof and a golden kalasha on the spire.

faqs.question: When is the temple open?
faqs.answer: The temple is open only between the months of April (Akshaya Tritiya) and November (Kartik Purnima).

name: Somnath Temple
description: The Somnath temple located in Prabhas Patan near Veraval in Saurashtra on the western coast of Gujarat, is believed to be the first among the twelve Jyotirlinga shrines of Shiva.
history: The temple was reconstructed several times in the past after repeated destruction by several Muslim invaders and rulers.
architecture: Chalukya style architecture, built with intricately carved stone.
address: Somnath Mandir Rd, Prabhas Patan, Gujarat 362268
dressCode: Traditional attire. Belts and electronics not allowed inside.

faqs.question: Are electronics allowed?
faqs.answer: No, mobile phones and cameras are prohibited inside the premises.

name: Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga
description: Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva and is one of the twelve Jyotirlingas, shrines which are said to be the most sacred abodes of Lord Shiva.
history: The presiding deity, Lord Shiva in the lingam form is believed to be Swayambhu, deriving currents of power from within itself.
architecture: Maratha, Bhumija and Chalukya styles of architecture with a massive spire and beautifully carved pillars.
dressCode: Dhoti for men and Saree for women during Bhasma Aarti.

faqs.question: What is Bhasma Aarti?
faqs.answer: It is the most famous aarti performed early in the morning using sacred ash.

name: Brihadeeswara Temple
description: Brihadeeswara Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva located in Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu. It is one of the largest South Indian temples and an exemplary example of fully realized Dravidian architecture.
history: Built by Raja Raja Chola I between 1003 and 1010 AD. The temple is a part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site known as the Great Living Chola Temples.
architecture: Dravidian architecture carved entirely out of granite with a vimana standing 216 ft tall.
address: Membalam Rd, Balaganapathy Nagar, Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu 613001

faqs.question: Is there an entry fee?
faqs.answer: Entry to the temple is free for all devotees and tourists.

name: Konark Sun Temple
description: Konark Sun Temple is a 13th-century CE Sun temple at Konark about 35 kilometres northeast from Puri on the coastline of Odisha.
history: Built by King Narasimhadeva I of the Eastern Ganga Dynasty in 1250 CE. It was designed in the shape of a colossal chariot.
architecture: Kalinga architecture in the form of a gigantic chariot with 24 elaborately carved wheels pulled by seven horses.
dressCode: Comfortable clothing, mostly visited as a monument.

faqs.question: Can we enter the main sanctum?
faqs.answer: The main sanctum is filled with sand to prevent its collapse, but visitors can explore the massive outer structure.

name: Lingaraja Temple
description: Lingaraja Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Harihara, a form of Shiva and Vishnu, and is one of the oldest temples in Bhubaneswar, the capital of Odisha.
history: Built by King Jajati Keshari in the 11th century CE. It is the most prominent landmark of Bhubaneswar city.
architecture: Kalinga style of architecture representing its quintessence with a central tower 180 ft tall.
address: Lingaraj Temple Rd, Old Town, Bhubaneswar, Odisha 751002
dressCode: Traditional Indian attire. Non-Hindus are not allowed inside.

faqs.question: Is photography allowed?
faqs.answer: No, strictly no photography inside the temple premises.
